(born 1 July 1978) in Tofoa, Tonga, is a [Tongan](/wiki/Tonga "Tonga") [rugby union](/wiki/Rugby_union "Rugby union") [international](/wiki/Tonga_national_rugby_union_team "Tonga national rugby union team") player. His position is [hooker](/wiki/Hooker_(rugby_union) "Hooker (rugby union)"). He is currently playing club rugby for [Ampthill](/wiki/Ampthill_RUFC "Ampthill RUFC") in [RFU Championship](/wiki/RFU_Championship "RFU Championship").

["Aviva Premiership Rugby - Worcester Warriors"](https://web.archive.org/web/20120207055710/http://www.premiershiprugby.com/clubs/worcester_warriors.php?section=1). *web page*. Premier Rugby. Archived from [the original](http://www.premiershiprugby.com/clubs/worcester_warriors.php?section=1) on 7 February 2012. Retrieved 26 September 2011.

["Veteran Tonga hooker agrees to join Gloucester from Edinburgh"](http://www1.skysports.com/rugby-union/news/12549/9355267/aviva-premiership-gloucester-sign-aleki-lutui-from-edinburgh). Sky Sports. 20 June 2014.

["Rugby Union: Aleki Lutui joins Ampthill but Joe Bercis leaves for Moseley"](http://www.bedfordshire-news.co.uk/rugby-union-aleki-lutui-joins-ampthill-joe-bercis/story-26523477-detail/story.html). Bedfordshire on Sunday. 18 May 2015.

["RGS Worcester pupil and daughter of Aleki Lutui picked for England"](https://www.worcesternews.co.uk/news/23294480.rgs-worcester-pupil-daughter-aleki-lutui-picked-england/?callback=in&code=MWIYMJNKNZGTNJQ1YI0ZNGIZLTG1ZGITYMMXNDHJODU2NJC3&state=221d0d8f2b98476ca8851f3608892f77). *Worcester News*. 2 February 2023. Retrieved 11 March 2025.

**Aleki Lutui** (born 1 July 1978) in Tofoa, Tonga, is a [Tongan](/wiki/Tonga "Tonga") [rugby union](/wiki/Rugby_union "Rugby union") [international](/wiki/Tonga_national_rugby_union_team "Tonga national rugby union team") player. His position is [hooker](/wiki/Hooker_(rugby_union) "Hooker (rugby union)"). He is currently playing club rugby for [Ampthill](/wiki/Ampthill_RUFC "Ampthill RUFC") in [RFU Championship](/wiki/RFU_Championship "RFU Championship").

He previously played for [Chiefs](/wiki/Chiefs_(Super_rugby_franchise) "Chiefs (Super rugby franchise)") in the international [Super 14](/wiki/Super_Rugby "Super Rugby") competition, as well as the [Bay of Plenty](/wiki/Bay_of_Plenty_Rugby_Union "Bay of Plenty Rugby Union") in the [Air New Zealand Cup](/wiki/Air_New_Zealand_Cup "Air New Zealand Cup"). He was also the top try scorer for the [Bay of Plenty](/wiki/Bay_of_Plenty_Rugby_Union "Bay of Plenty Rugby Union") in the 2002 National Provincial Championship (now, the [Air New Zealand Cup](/wiki/Air_New_Zealand_Cup "Air New Zealand Cup")).

He has appeared numerous times for his national team [Tonga](/wiki/Tonga_national_rugby_union_team "Tonga national rugby union team"). He made his debut for [Tonga](/wiki/Tonga_national_rugby_union_team "Tonga national rugby union team") in March 1999 in a match against [Georgia](/wiki/Georgia_national_rugby_union_team "Georgia national rugby union team").

He played for [Tonga](/wiki/Tonga_national_rugby_union_team "Tonga national rugby union team") in their 2001 Pacific Rim campaign and later in the year toured Scotland and Wales. On June 5, 2004, he captained [Tonga](/wiki/Tonga_national_rugby_union_team "Tonga national rugby union team") for the first time in a match against [Fiji](/wiki/Fiji_national_rugby_union_team "Fiji national rugby union team"). In June 2005 he captained [Tonga](/wiki/Tonga_national_rugby_union_team "Tonga national rugby union team") in four matches; two against both [Fiji](/wiki/Fiji_national_rugby_union_team "Fiji national rugby union team") and [Samoa](/wiki/Samoa_national_rugby_union_team "Samoa national rugby union team").

He went on tour with the [Pacific Islanders rugby union team](/wiki/Pacific_Islanders_rugby_union_team "Pacific Islanders rugby union team") in 2004 for a series of Tests against a number of southern rugby nations. He was included in the Pacific Nations tour to Europe in late 2006 and also played in the game against England at Twickenham in 2008.

Lutui joined Worcester in September 2006 on a two-year deal. The Tongan international was a former police officer before he took up rugby.

He has been a key figure for previous clubs in [Super 14](/wiki/Super_Rugby "Super Rugby"). He has since become an player [Sixways](/wiki/Sixways_Stadium "Sixways Stadium").

Lutui, a ball carrier and hitter, was named in the Tonga squad for the [2007 Rugby World Cup](/wiki/2007_Rugby_World_Cup "2007 Rugby World Cup") in France, the [2011 Rugby World Cup](/wiki/2011_Rugby_World_Cup "2011 Rugby World Cup") in New Zealand and the [2015 Rugby World Cup](/wiki/2015_Rugby_World_Cup "2015 Rugby World Cup") in England.

On the 24 April 2013, Lutui signed a two-year deal which will bring him to [Edinburgh Rugby](/wiki/Edinburgh_Rugby "Edinburgh Rugby") in the [Pro12](/wiki/Pro14 "Pro14").

However, on 20 June 2014, Lutui left Edinburgh to return to the Premiership to join [Gloucester Rugby](/wiki/Gloucester_Rugby "Gloucester Rugby") for next season's campaign.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

For the 2015–16 season, Lutui signed for [Ampthill](/wiki/Ampthill_RUFC "Ampthill RUFC") playing in [National League 1](/wiki/National_League_1 "National League 1"), joining a strong contingent of [Tongan](/wiki/Tonga "Tonga") players.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Aleki_Lutui&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Personal life")]

His daughter [Haineala Lutui](/wiki/Haineala_Lutui "Haineala Lutui") is also a rugby union player.[[4]](#cite_note-4)
